name: Code Generation
description: Evaluates generated code on correctness, quality, completeness, and efficiency.

perspectives.name: Correctness
perspectives.description: Code is syntactically and logically correct
perspectives.criteria: Is the generated code syntactically valid and logically correct for the task?
perspectives.scoringGuide: 1=Broken code, 2=Major bugs, 3=Minor bugs, 4=Correct, 5=Perfectly correct and robust

perspectives.name: Code Quality
perspectives.criteria: Is the code well-structured, readable, and following language idioms?

perspectives.name: Completeness
perspectives.criteria: Does the code implement all the requested functionality?
perspectives.scoringGuide: 1=Very incomplete, 2=Missing major parts, 3=Mostly complete, 4=Complete, 5=Complete with edge cases

perspectives.name: Efficiency
perspectives.criteria: Is the code reasonably efficient in time and space complexity?
